---
title: "LRPGF04: Gain factors for letter sounds (L)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
treatment_marginal = pd.read_csv(_here / "treatment_marginal.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPGF04 is a **gain-factors** model for letter sounds (`yarclet`): what is associated with how much children gain across the three period transitions (ANCOVA - the period post-count given its pre-count - Beta-Binomial logit, child random intercept). It conditions on the own baseline, age, cognitive ability (block design), no upstream skills (age / ability dominate per the DAG), with focal interactions group$\times$ability, group$\times$own-baseline, age$\times$ability.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

**Only the randomised on-intervention term is causal** (identified by the period-1 contrast; reproduces the ITT effect). Every other coefficient is an **adjusted association** confounded by latent general ability, repaired up to shrinkage by the child random intercept - report as "associated with", never "drives". Intervention sessions/dose are never conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded (not a DAG node; statistically redundant).

:::

## Model

Beta-Binomial$(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$ on the period post-count, $N = 32$, with
$\operatorname{logit}\mu = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \beta_{\text{trt}}\text{OnIntervention} + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(\text{pre}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}) + \sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}$. Priors: shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (treatment)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=24%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

`role` flags the randomised on-intervention term **causal** and all else **association**; `mean`/`lo`/`hi` are the posterior mean and equal-tailed 95% CrI (logit scale); `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef}>0)$. Interactions are expected to be weakly identified (wide CrI) at $n\approx54$.

### Items-scale treatment effect

```{python}
# | echo: false
treatment_marginal
```

Average marginal effect of being on intervention on the probability scale (`trt_prob_*`) and the items scale (`trt_items_*`, out of 32).

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
